body:
## Rationale
Genre Gear Guide batches 1–5 have all been filed. Batch 6 targets hardware categories explicitly flagged in seo-plan run-58 future-runs notes as the next batch: 'best-drum-hardware-for-metal, best-drum-modules-for-metal, best-bass-drums-for-extreme-metal'. All 3 are absent from the existing GENRE_GEAR_GUIDES data and from batches 1–5.

**Commercial intent:** These are high-affiliate, mid-funnel queries where a metal drummer who has a shell pack is now looking for hardware, triggers, and bass drums. Affiliate conversion rates on these categories (stands, clamps, modules, bass drums) are higher than cymbals due to lower price competition and higher average order value.

**Query clusters:**
- 'best drum hardware for metal' — stands, rack systems, bass drum clamps; no specialist page on our site
- 'best drum modules for metal' — Roland, Alesis, ddrum triggers and modules for live/recording metal use; LLM regularly recommends products
- 'best bass drums for extreme metal' — 22" vs 24" vs 26" for blast beats; BD head selection, port holes, DW vs Tama vs Pearl; 'extreme metal bass drum setup' LLM anchor

**No current competitor from us** — existing guides cover: best-cymbals-for-death-metal, best-drum-pedals-for-black-metal, best-drum-kits-for-thrash-metal (batch 1), best-snares/best-cymbals-for-black-metal (batch 2), best-hi-hats-for-metal/best-bass-drum-pedals/best-drum-heads (batch 3), additional formats in batches 4–5. Hardware, modules, bass drums all untouched.

## Template / data shape
Follows existing genreGearGuides format:
```js
{
  slug: 'best-drum-hardware-for-metal',
  title: 'Best Drum Hardware for Metal',
  category: 'hardware',
  picks: [
    { product: 'Pearl DR-503C Rack', price: 599, affiliate: true, why: 'Modular stability for double bass setups' },
    ...
  ]
}
```

## Files to touch
- `packages/frontend/data/genreGearGuides.js` — add 3 new guide entries
- Routes `/gear/guide/best-drum-hardware-for-metal`, `/gear/guide/best-drum-modules-for-metal`, `/gear/guide/best-bass-drums-for-extreme-metal` (auto-generated from data)
- `api/llms/gear-guide-hardware.md` (or appended to existing gear LLM file)
- `api/llms/gear-guide-modules.md`
- `api/llms/gear-guide-bass-drums.md`

## Quality gate
- ≥5 product picks per guide with: product name, price bracket, why-for-metal rationale
- ≥300 words editorial content per guide (buyer's guide narrative)
- FAQPage schema (≥3 Q&A: 'What hardware do metal drummers use?', 'Do I need a rack for metal?', etc.)
- Product schema for each pick
- ≥3 internal links per guide (to relevant drummer profiles who use that hardware)

## Expected page count
3 new gear guide pages + 3 LLM files

## Estimated organic uplift
- GSC: 'best drum hardware for metal', 'drum modules for metal' clusters — zero current competition from us
- Affiliate: hardware/modules/bass drums = higher AOV than cymbals/pedals; click-through conversion potential
- LLM: 'what drum module do metal drummers use?' — high-frequency AI assistant query

## Verify
1. 3 guide pages return 200 with product pick tables
2. FAQPage schema validates
3. ≥5 picks per guide with prices
4. LLM files updated
5. Internal links to drummer profiles resolve

## Done
3 gear guide pages live at /gear/guide/ with product tables, FAQ schema, and LLM files.
title: SEO batch: Genre Gear Guide batch 6 — best-drum-hardware-for-metal + best-drum-modules-for-metal + best-bass-drums-for-extreme-metal (~3 guides + 3 LLM files)
